titleStmt.title: ODD for the Digital Edition of Filippo Cavriana's Correspondence
titleStmt.author: Clément Godbarge

availability.licence: This work is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License.

sourceDesc.p: Born-digital ODD document for the CavrianaCorr project.

docTitle.titlePart: ODD for the Digital Edition of Filippo Cavriana's Correspondence (1568–1606)

div.head: Project Description
div.p: This ODD defines the TEI customisation for the digital edition of the correspondence of Filippo Cavriana (1536–1606), an Italian physician, humanist, and diplomatic informant active at the French court during the Wars of Religion. The edition publishes letters preserved in the Archivio di Stato di Firenze (Mediceo del Principato), the Archivio di Stato di Mantova (Archivio Gonzaga), and the Bibliothèque nationale de France.

Cavriana wrote under his own name and under the pseudonym Vincenzo da Castello. His letters are addressed primarily to Medicean secretaries (Bartolomeo Concini, Jacopo Dani) and to the Duke of Mantua (Guglielmo Gonzaga). Several letters contain passages in numerical cipher, deciphered either by contemporary hands or by the editor.

div.head: Transcription Policy
div.p: The transcription is semi-diplomatic: the original spelling, punctuation, and capitalisation of the source are preserved. No silent normalisation is applied. Line breaks are recorded with lb to maintain the spatial structure of the manuscript page. Page and folio breaks are marked with pb.

Abbreviations are expanded using the choice / abbr / expan mechanism, making both the original abbreviated form and its resolution available to the reader. The original abbreviation marks — combining diacritics such as the combining dot above (U+0315), the combining macron (U+0304), and special letter forms (ꝑ, ꝗ) — are preserved in the abbr element.

div.head: Named Entity Annotation
div.p: All persons and places mentioned in the main hand of each letter are annotated with persName and placeName elements carrying a ref attribute that points to a centralised authority file (persNames.xml and placeNames.xml). Entity annotation is restricted to the main scribal hand; marginalia and secondary hands are not annotated.
Person entries in the authority file include VIAF identifiers where available. Place entries include Getty TGN identifiers and geographic coordinates.

div.head: Cipher Text
div.p: Several letters contain passages in numerical substitution cipher. These are encoded as seg elements with type=cipher and subtype=numeric. Where a contemporary hand has written the decipherment above the cipher text in the manuscript, this is recorded with add elements of type=decipher and place=above. Where the editor supplies a decipherment, supplied is used with reason=deciphered, resp=#editor, and a cert value indicating the level of confidence.

div.head: Damage, Gaps, and Unclear Readings
div.p: Physical damage to the manuscript (typically ink corrosion) is recorded with damage and gap. The damage element carries an agent attribute (e.g. ink_corrosion). The enclosed gap specifies the estimated extent with quantity and unit (typically words).
Text that can be read but with uncertainty is marked with unclear. Where the reason for the uncertainty is known, a reason attribute is used (e.g. illegible).
Where the editor restores text lost to damage, supplied is used with reason=damage.

div.head: Corrections
div.p: Where the scribe has made a clear error (not a variant spelling characteristic of the period), the choice / sic / corr mechanism is used. Period-typical spellings (e.g. percioche for perciocché) are left unemended.

div.head: File Structure
div.p: Each letter is encoded in a separate TEI file named by its date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD.xml). Where multiple letters share the same date, a letter suffix is appended (e.g. 1570-11-02-a.xml).

div.head: Header Structure
div.p: Every letter file contains a teiHeader with the following components:

div.head: fileDesc
div.p: The titleStmt records the title (in the form Letter from [Author] to [Recipient], [Date]), the author, the editor (Clément Godbarge), and the institutional affiliation (University of St Andrews).
The publicationStmt declares the publisher, place of publication (St Andrews, Scotland, UK), date, and the CC BY 4.0 licence.

div.head: encodingDesc
profileDesc
Text Body Structure
div.p: The body of each letter is enclosed in a single div type=letter and follows the epistolary structure:

list.item: pb: page/folio break with n giving the folio designation (e.g. 235r, 235v);
opener / salute: the opening salutation;
p: body paragraphs with inline encoding;
closer: containing salute (closing formula), dateline (place and date), and signed (signature, sometimes with type=alias on persName for pseudonymous signatures);
postscript: post-scriptum paragraphs, when present.

div.head: Abbreviations
div.p: All abbreviations are encoded with choice containing abbr (the abbreviation as it appears in the manuscript, including combining diacritics) and expan (the expanded form).

Common patterns include:
Honorific titles: V.S. → Vostra Signoria; S.or → Signor; Ill.mo → Illustrissimo
Nasal abbreviations with combining dot: co̍ → con; no̍ → non
Superlative suffixes: -iss.o → -issimo
Pronoun abbreviations: q̍sto → questo; q̄lli → quelli
Per/par abbreviations with special forms: p̸ → per; ꝑ → par

div.head: Named Entities
div.p: persName elements carry a ref attribute pointing to the authority file (e.g. #pers-cavriana-f, #pers-medici-cos-1). The naming convention is #pers-[surname]-[disambiguator].
placeName elements carry a ref attribute pointing to the place authority file (e.g. #place-paris, #place-firenze). The naming convention is #place-[toponym].
When Cavriana uses a pseudonym or alias to refer to himself or others, the persName element carries type=alias in addition to the ref.

div.head: Line and Page Breaks
div.p: lb marks every line break in the manuscript. When a word is broken across a line boundary, the lb at the break point carries break=no, indicating that no space should be inserted between the word fragments.
pb marks folio or page boundaries, with n giving the folio designation in the format NNNr or NNNv (recto/verso).

div.head: Cipher Passages
div.p: Cipher text is encoded as follows:

list.item: The cipher itself is wrapped in seg with xml:id (e.g. cyph1), type=cipher, subtype=numeric, and hand identifying the scribal hand.

Contemporary decipherments written above the cipher in the manuscript are encoded as add with xml:id (e.g. dec1), type=decipher, hand (typically #hand2), place=above, and target pointing to the cipher xml:id.
Editorial decipherments are encoded with supplied carrying reason=deciphered, resp=#editor, and cert (high or medium).

div.head: Damage and Gaps
div.p: damage with agent (e.g. ink_corrosion) wraps the affected area. Inside, gap records the extent of lost text with reason=hole, quantity, and unit=words.

div.head: Unclear Readings
div.p: unclear wraps text that can be partially read but remains uncertain. Where the cause of illegibility is known, a reason attribute is used.

div.head: Editorial Notes
div.p: note elements with type=editorial are used for the editor's commentary on factual matters, identification issues, or historical context. Notes with type=summary appear in the header to provide a brief summary of each letter's content.

div.head: Split Words
div.p: When a word is split across a structural boundary (e.g. a page break falling within a word that also coincides with a paragraph break), seg with type=split and part (I for initial, M for medial, F for final) is used.

div.head: Person Authority (persNames.xml)
div.p: Each person is encoded as a person element with xml:id within a listPerson. The entry contains:

list.item: sex with value (male, female, unknown);
birth and death: dates when known;
idno type=VIAF: the VIAF identifier.

div.p: Persons are grouped thematically (Italian noble houses, French royal family, Medicean officials, etc.). Entries that require further research are placed in a dedicated Incomplete Entities section.

div.head: Place Authority (placeNames.xml)
div.p: Each place is encoded as a place element with xml:id within a listPlace. The entry contains:

list.item: placeName with type=modern and xml:lang=en: the modern English name;
placeName with type=country or region: broader geographic context;
location / geo: geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude);
idno type=TGN: the Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names URI.

div.head: Hand Descriptions
div.p: Each letter's handDesc records the number of hands (hands attribute) and identifies each with a handNote:

list.item: xml:id=hand1: the primary hand (usually Cavriana's autograph, or an unknown copyist);
xml:id=hand2: a secondary hand, typically used for cipher decipherments or recipient annotations;
scope: sole (only hand), major (primary hand in multi-hand documents), or minor (secondary hand).

div.head: Schema Specification
div.p: The following schemaSpec defines the TEI customisation for this project. It selects the required modules and constrains element and attribute usage to match the encoding practices documented above.

moduleRef.include: p name date note choice abbr expan unclear gap add del lb pb sic corr foreign desc hi list item ref head respStmt resp author editor title publisher pubPlace availability licence signed salute
teiHeader fileDesc titleStmt publicationStmt sourceDesc encodingDesc editorialDecl projectDesc profileDesc correspDesc correspAction
TEI text body div front back titlePage docTitle titlePart docAuthor docDate opener closer dateline postscript
msDesc msIdentifier settlement repository collection idno msContents msItem locus physDesc objectDesc layoutDesc handDesc handNote supportDesc condition textLang
persName placeName listPerson person listPlace place sex birth death occupation geo location
att gi val schemaSpec moduleRef elementSpec classSpec attDef valList valItem

valItem.desc: A single letter.
A passage written in cipher.
A fragment of a word split across a structural boundary.
A numerical substitution cipher.
A contemporary decipherment of a cipher passage.
Written above the line or cipher text.
Text deciphered from a cipher passage by the editor.
Text reconstructed by the editor where the manuscript is damaged.
Text supplied where material is missing from the manuscript.

attDef.desc: Identifies who supplied the text. Typically #editor.

valItem.desc: High confidence in the supplied reading.
Moderate confidence in the supplied reading.
Low confidence in the supplied reading.
Damage caused by iron gall ink corrosion.
Physical hole in the manuscript.
Material missing (e.g. lost page).
Text present but entirely illegible.
Extent measured in words.
Extent measured in characters.
Extent measured in lines.
A brief summary of the letter's content (in teiHeader).
An editorial comment by the editor.
A marginal note in the manuscript.
The object is a letter.
The only hand in the document.
The primary hand in a multi-hand document.
A secondary hand.
The action of sending the letter.
The action of receiving the letter.
An alternative name, pseudonym, or title used in the letters.
The historical toponym.
The modern name of the place.
The country in which the place is located.
The region in which the place is located.
A member of the nobility.
A member of the clergy or religious order.
A diplomatic agent or ambassador.
A medical practitioner.
A military officer or soldier.
A secretary or administrative official.
Virtual International Authority File identifier.
Getty Thesaurus of Geographic Names URI.
Digital Object Identifier.
Male.
Female.
Sex unknown or unspecified.
